commit.message:
gpsd-proxy: replace custom Go daemon with gpsd package

Use the battle-tested gpsd daemon directly instead of maintaining
custom Go NMEA parsing code. Simplifies the addon to just installing
gpsd and running it against the configured device.

- Remove all Go source (main.go, go.mod, go.sum)
- Dockerfile now just installs gpsd via apk
- run.sh invokes: gpsd -N -n -G -S 2947 $DEVICE
- config.yaml reduced to device selection only
- Bump version to 1.1.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cfl: seed 4 printer profiles into 7 fuzzer corpora (28 seeds)

COVERAGE GAP: Zero printer (prtr) class profiles existed across all 13 CFL
fuzzer corpora. Printer profiles exercise critical code paths:
- AToB/BToA LUT pairs (mft1 type) with 3D CLUT grids (17^3 and 33^3)
- Gamut mapping tag (gamt) — 3-to-1 channel reduction
- CMYK color space input handling (4-channel vs 3-channel)
- v5.0 profile features (CMYK-3DLUTs2)

Seeds chosen for maximum diversity:
- Tek350Monaco2.icc: v2.0 RGB->Lab, 405KB, Monaco CMM, 10 tags
- SC_paper_eci.icc: v2.4 CMYK->Lab, 1.8MB, ECI standard, 13 tags
- CMYK-3DLUTs2.icc: v5.0 CMYK->Lab, 1.8MB, iccMAX 3D LUTs, 10 tags
- cve-2023-46602.icc: v2.1 CMYK->Lab, 1.6MB, malformed CVE PoC, 12 tags

roles/run_benchmarks/tasks/main.yml:

  (uses community.general.make instead of ansible.builtin.package)

playbooks/run_benchmarks.yml:

  works against statically-inventoried hosts without dynamic inventory
- Add when: hypervisor_host != '' guard to all 5 hypervisor health check tasks

galaxy.yml:
- Replace all template placeholder values (author, description, URLs, tags)
  with real project values; pin ansible.utils dependency range >=2.0.0,<7.0.0

  [ssh_connection] where it takes effect for SSH connections

plugins/:

  (contained placeholder/broken imports, served no functional purpose)

scripts/generate_benchmark_report.py:
- Fix O(n²) lookup_passmark: replace list comprehension over all CSV entries
  with single-pass linear scan (O(n) once, O(1) on exact match)

commit.message:
chore(renovate): fix automerge rules and reschedule lts cron (#1171)

## Summary

Two fixes to improve automation on the `main` branch and LTS release
schedule.

### Fix Renovate automerge rules (`.github/renovate.json5`)

The previous `packageRules` had gaps — GitHub Actions digest/version
updates and the Justfile `regex` manager were not covered by any
`automerge: true` rule, causing those PRs to require manual queue entry
even after approval.

Replaces 3 fragmented rules with 2 clean ones:
- All `digest`/`pin`/`pinDigest` updates get `automerge: true` across
all managers
- GitHub Actions `minor`/`patch` version bumps also get `automerge:
true`

### Reschedule LTS release cron

Changes the weekly LTS build trigger from Sunday 2 AM UTC to Tuesday 6
AM UTC (1 AM EST / 2 AM EDT).

commit.message:
Add MusicBrainz bulk match script for backfilling missing MBIDs (#338)

* Initial plan

* Add MusicBrainz bulk match script for finding/fixing missing MBIDs

New files:
- bin/musicbrainz-bulk-match.ts — main script (connects to Payload, queries collections, calls MusicBrainz API)
- bin/musicbrainz-bulk-match-utils.ts — shared types, CLI parsing, classification logic
- bin/musicbrainz-bulk-match.test.ts — 29 unit tests for utils

Supports: --collection, --fix, --verify, --limit flags
Added yarn musicbrainz:match script to package.json

Co-authored-by: tjnicolaides <1401894+tjnicolaides@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix musicbrainz:match script to use preload-nextenv-fix.mjs

Without the --import ./bin/preload-nextenv-fix.mjs flag, the script crashes
immediately with 'Cannot destructure property loadEnvConfig' due to the
known @next/env ESM/CJS interop issue. All other Payload CLI scripts
already use this preload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix four issues with MusicBrainz bulk match script

1. Release search: use field-specific Lucene syntax (release:"title"
   AND artist:"name"), increase result limit from 5 to 10, lower score
   threshold from 85 to 75. Records matching improved from 0/28 to 10/28.

2. Verify mode: add 'verified' status for records whose existing MBID
   matches the API lookup, instead of silently classifying them as
   'skipped'. Shows ✔️ icon and counter in reports.

3. Replace deprecated getPayloadHMR with getPayload from 'payload'.

4. Add preload-nextenv-fix.mjs to the yarn script (already pushed in
   prior commit).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add encoding repair and fallback search to bulk match script

Encoding repair: detect and fix mojibake (UTF-8 decoded as Latin-1)
before searching. Recovers records like Poliça, Björk, Liberación.

Fallback search: when artist+title search returns nothing, retry with
title only. Catches misspelled artist names like Frank Ferdinand.

Records matching improved from 10/28 to 15/28.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Adopt React 18 `useTransition` for heavy table filtering (#2044)

Replace `useDeferredValue` with `useTransition` in the three largest
cluster management pages to expose the `isPending` flag, enabling a
subtle visual pending state while table filtering catches up to search
input.

### Changes

- **`DataStreamsPage.tsx`** — swap both `useDeferredValue(search)` and
`useDeferredValue(fieldSearch)` for `useTransition` + local deferred
state; add opacity fade on `TableContainer` while pending
- **`IndicesPage.tsx`** — same pattern for the single search value
- **`IndexTemplatesPage.tsx`** — same pattern; wrap template tables in a
`Box` with pending opacity
- **`NodesPage.tsx`** — no changes (no search filtering exists)

### Pattern

```tsx
const [isFilterPending, startTransition] = useTransition();
const [deferredSearch, setDeferredSearch] = useState(search);

useEffect(() => {
  startTransition(() => setDeferredSearch(search));
}, [search, startTransition]);

// Subtle fade while the table re-filters
```

Add `breeze pr auto-triage` command (#62682)

Add a new Breeze CLI command that helps maintainers efficiently triage
open PRs from non-collaborators that don't meet minimum quality criteria.

The command fetches open PRs via GitHub GraphQL API with optimized chunked
queries, runs deterministic CI checks (failures, merge conflicts, missing
test workflows), optionally runs LLM-based quality assessment, and presents
flagged PRs interactively for maintainer review with author profiles and
contribution history.

Key features:
- Optimized GraphQL queries with chunking to avoid GitHub timeout errors
- Deterministic CI failure detection with categorized fix instructions
- LLM assessment via `claude` or `codex` CLI for content quality
- Interactive review with Rich panels, clickable links, and author context
- "maintainer-accepted" label to skip PRs on future runs
- Workflow approval support for first-time contributor PRs awaiting CI runs
- Merge conflict and commits-behind detection with rebase guidance

Update dev/breeze/src/airflow_breeze/utils/llm_utils.py

commit.message:
Clear stale transform rows on refresh error (#2060)

`TransformsPage` persists the last successful fetch in state and falls
back to it on error, leaving stale rows visible and interactive
alongside the error alert.

**Fix:** Clear `lastSuccessfulTransforms` when result status transitions
to `"error"`:

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  if (result.status === "success") {
    setLastSuccessfulTransforms(result.data);
  } else if (result.status === "error") {
    setLastSuccessfulTransforms([]);
  }
}, [result]);
```

- **`TransformsPage.tsx`** — reset cached transforms on error so the
table unmounts and only the error alert renders
- **`TransformsPage.test.tsx`** — add test: success → refresh error →
stale row absent

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2051

commit.message:
Implement Phase 9.1: APPLICATION LIFECYCLE - Public wain API

This commit implements the first milestone of the public API infrastructure:

**Public API (app.go):**
- Created wain package at module root as primary public API entry point
- Implemented App type with full lifecycle management (546 LOC)
- NewApp() and NewAppWithConfig() for application initialization
- Run() method with blocking event loop
- Quit() method for graceful shutdown
- Display server auto-detection: Wayland preferred, X11 fallback
- Renderer auto-detection: Intel GPU → AMD GPU → software fallback
- Event loop management with single-goroutine dispatch
- Graceful resource cleanup (renderer, connections)

**API Features:**
- DisplayServer type (Wayland, X11, Unknown)
- AppConfig with Width, Height, ForceSoftware, ForceX11, DRMPath, Verbose
- DefaultConfig() helper
- BackendType() and DisplayServer() getters
- Dimensions() query method

**Demo Application (cmd/wain-demo):**
- Demonstrates public API usage (52 LOC)
- Signal handling for graceful shutdown (SIGINT, SIGTERM)
- Reports final state (display server, backend type, dimensions)

**Build Integration:**
- Added wain-demo target to Makefile
- Static binary build verified (7.5 MB)
- ldd check: "not a dynamic executable"

**Validation:**
- All 59 test packages passing (100% pass rate)
- Zero regressions in existing code metrics
- Phase 9.1 milestone achieved: opens blank window on both X11/Wayland

commit.message:
fix(game): eliminate Redis blocking in tick loop with state caching

- Add in-memory state cache (stateCache Map) so tick loop reads from
  cache instead of awaiting Redis GET every tick
- Replace blocking repository.save() in tick with fire-and-forget
  scheduleSave/drainSave (latest-wins pattern, same as broadcasts)
- Replace scheduleBroadcast/drainBroadcast in tick with direct
  void emitDistributed() calls to avoid broadcastInFlight timing
  issues between consecutive ticks
- Restore missing directory.setOwner() call in createRoom that was
  accidentally removed
- Cache-aware getState(), disconnectPlayer(), removePlayer(),
  handleDistributedCommand() all check stateCache first
- Cache cleared on room deletion (removePlayer when last player leaves)
- resetStaleMatchIfNeeded made synchronous (returns boolean)

The tick loop is now effectively synchronous: Map.get + game logic +
scheduleSave + void emitDistributed ≈ <1ms per room, eliminating the
10-50ms Redis latency spikes that caused game freezes during boss fights.

Closes #15

commit.message:
feat: Metadata Capability Graph subsystem (#1259)

- Add CapabilityNode/CapabilityEdge/EntityDescriptor compact structs
- CapabilityType enum: QueryEntity, CreateEntity, UpdateEntity, DeleteEntity,
  RunAction, RunWorkflow, NavigateView, TraverseRelationship
- MetadataCapabilityGraph with integer-indexed adjacency lists (forward + reverse)
  for O(1) neighbor lookup and cache-friendly traversal
- CapabilityGraphBuilder: derives graph from RuntimeEntityRegistry at startup
  - CRUD nodes per entity with natural cycle edges
  - RunAction nodes per action with query↔action edges
  - TraverseRelationship nodes from lookup/child-list/related-document fields

fix: accept /dev/mshv as hypervisor prerequisite in integration tests

The test helpers checked only for /dev/kvm, causing all VM-booting
integration tests to skip on MSHV hosts. Now accepts either
/dev/kvm or /dev/mshv.

Validated on Azure Linux 3 with MSHV kernel (6.6.100.mshv1-3.azl3):
- All 13 integration tests pass
- Guest boot + agent ready: 178ms
- vCPU resize: OK
- VM pool warm: OK
